title: cotoneaster affinis var. obtusus
intro: Cotoneaster affinis var. obtusus, also known as the shrub cotoneaster, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to the Himalayas and China. The plant's leaves and fruits have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Cotoneaster affinis var. obtusus has been reported to have various health benefits, including the ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antiseptic. The plant's leaves can be used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
